planTitle: Drive Resilience and Finalization State Model

content: Drive finalization infrastructure failures are represented as Drive run/task outcomes (`finalization_failed`) and events, not as a new global task status and not as behavioral `Blocked`. A verified task whose driver-owned commit or task-status finalization fails remains not Done/Blocked, with recovery truth in `pending-finalization.json`, events, and `run.completion.json`.

Persist pending finalization as a phase-specific recovery contract in the run workdir: commit pending state must include `headBeforeFinalization`, task-status pending state must include `commitSha`, and state-commit pending state must include the run `taskIds` plus `headBeforeFinalization`. Keep `blockedTaskId`/`blockedReason` reserved for behavioral blocks and use finalization-specific fields (`finalizationPhase`, `finalizationReason`, optional task/commit details, `pendingFinalizationPath`) in `DriverResult`.

CLI resume must handle `pending-finalization.json` before spawning any backend work. Retry the recorded finalization phase first, clear stale completion state and the pending file only after success, then recompute dirty-worktree safety before continuing remaining tasks; do not treat `task_finalization_failed` as task completion for resume slicing until a later `task_done` exists.

Do not accept externally completed state-commit finalization based only on a clean worktree and changed HEAD. Resume may clear pending `state_commit` only when every pending task ID still resolves through `TaskManager.getTask()` and currently has status `Done`; changed HEAD without current Done task files may be unrelated or may have reverted the task-state updates.

`stateCommitPolicy` defaults are owned by driver core, not individual frontends: resolve to `final-state-commit` when `commitPolicy` is `driver-commits`, otherwise `none`, unless an explicit override is provided. CLI Drive and Pi `run_driver` should pass overrides but rely on the shared resolver so successful run behavior cannot diverge by frontend.

Final Drive state commits are deliberately narrow: stage only this run's task markdown files under `missions/tasks/` for the run task IDs, use the project-root repository commit lock, and exclude source files, `missions/sessions/`, archive moves, reviews, and `memory/`. Source commits keep excluding `missions/**`, `memory/**`, and `.cosmonauts/*.lock`.

All Drive commit operations and finalization retries must acquire the repository commit lock from `spec.projectRoot`, not `cosmonautsRoot` or framework/package roots. Splitting lock roots between CLI and `run_driver` can allow concurrent commits in the same project and invalidate lock-file pathspec regression coverage.

Expose finalization as additive observable events instead of replacing existing Drive events. Emit `finalize` phase events plus `task_finalization_failed`, terminal `run_finalization_failed`, and `plan_completion_candidate`; bridge these through the activity bus, make `run_finalization_failed` stop detached event bridging, and surface finalization details in `watch_events`, `drive status`, and `drive list` without blocked-field overload.

Drive emits `plan_completion_candidate` when all tasks labeled `plan:<slug>` are Done after successful finalization, but it intentionally does not edit the plan, archive artifacts, distill memory, push, or open PRs. This gives coordinators useful completion evidence without moving lifecycle ownership into the driver.

Verification-only tasks with no source changes are a successful source-finalization case only when the report is structured success and postflight passed; emit commit finalization as skipped with reason `no_changes`, do not emit `commit_made`, and continue to task-status finalization. Preserve stricter handling for unknown reports so no-change inference does not silently complete ambiguous work.
